reviewTexts:
Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange is a MUST-stop in Pittsburgh if you’re even remotely into coffee. This place has been around since the 1980s and has built a reputation as one of the city’s go-to specialty coffee shops, and you can feel that history the second you walk in.
As soon as you open the door, you’re hit with that amazing fresh coffee bean smell. The staff is always super friendly, welcoming, and happy to help, which makes the experience even better.
One of the coolest parts of the shop is in the back, where they have a huge selection of whole beans from all over the world. You can smell each one and even bag your own beans, which is a really unique touch.
Another standout is their cold brew setup- they have multiple taps with different varieties, which makes it fun to mix things up instead of getting the same coffee every time. It’s a great way to try new flavors and find something you really like.
Prices are very reasonable, especially for the quality and experience you’re getting. It’s one of those spots that feels both local and special at the same time.
This is a place we make a point to stop at every time we’re in Pittsburgh- grab a coffee and then walk around the city. Highly recommend checking it out.
Smells are amazing! Staff are super friendly and accommodating. Loved the Laura Palmer on the hot April day. Can’t wait to go back!
Their espresso blend is hands down one of the best beans I've put through my espresso maker.

Food was excellent
Cameron was a  great server
Absolutely amazing you have to stop in here if you’re in Pittsburgh
The latter was amazing and the coffee bean scooping was super cool!

Most coffee gives me a terrible aftertaste and I had coffee at work and it turns out to be Allegheny coffees house blend.  It was the best I’ve ever had! Eric helped me out and went out of his way to make sure I had the grinder on the right setting. Thank you Eric!!
